title: Claude Code Best Practices & Token Optimization
description: Comprehensive guide to code quality, token efficiency, and agent workflows in Claude Code. Covers context management, TDD, code review processes, token economics, skills design, and subagent orchestration. Portuguese guide based on Claude Code internal architecture.
overview:
## Claude Code Setup Overview

A productivity-focused Claude Code configuration built around token efficiency, structured workflows, and UI quality — suitable for full-stack engineers who run Claude heavily and want consistent behavior across sessions.

**superpowers** enforces disciplined workflows (TDD, debugging, planning, parallel agents) through skills that run before Claude acts. **claude-mem** provides persistent cross-session memory so context isn't rebuilt each conversation. **token-optimizer** audits and reduces context waste, surfacing where tokens are being burned. **RTK** (via CLAUDE.md + shell hooks) transparently rewrites commands through a Rust proxy for 60–90% token savings on CLI operations.

**context7** pulls live library documentation so Claude isn't relying on stale training data. **frontend-design** guides Claude toward production-quality UI instead of generic scaffolding. **playwright** enables real browser automation and UI verification without leaving the agent loop. **github** connects Claude to PR and issue workflows directly.

**claude-hud** displays a live status line with session metrics and context quality score. **plugin-dev** provides structured workflows for building and validating new plugins. **snapshot** lets the entire setup be exported and applied to another machine. **tray-api** integrates with Tray's automation platform.

Mirror this setup if you want reduced token spend, enforced engineering discipline, and a Claude that remembers how you work.
